pattern: A greedy post-order labels each node as covered, holding a camera, or needing one, placing a camera only when a child is left uncovered — pushing cameras as high as possible.
hints: A camera covers itself, its parent, and its direct children, so leaves are never the best place to put one — their parents are.
Process children before the node and track three states per node: it has a camera, it is covered by a child's camera, or it is uncovered and forces a camera above it.

Post-order: if any child is uncovered, place a camera here (count++) and mark this node as having one; else if any child has a camera, this node is covered; otherwise it is uncovered. After the recursion, if the root ends uncovered, add one final camera.

edge_inputs.description: single node still needs a camera
root with two leaf children, camera goes on the root
balanced depth-two tree, cameras on the middle layer

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    left = [None] * n
    right = [None] * n
    open_slots = [(0, 'L'), (0, 'R')]
    for child in range(1, n):
        idx = rng.randrange(len(open_slots))
        parent, side = open_slots.pop(idx)
        if side == 'L':
            left[parent] = child
        else:
            right[parent] = child
        open_slots.append((child, 'L'))
        open_slots.append((child, 'R'))
    out = []
    queue = [0]
    while queue:
        idx = queue.pop(0)
        if idx is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(0)
        if left[idx] is not None or right[idx] is not None:
            queue.append(left[idx])
            queue.append(right[idx])
    return [out]
stress.note: all values are 0 per the constraint

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minCameraCover(self, root):
        # Independent approach: bottom-up DP returning, for each subtree, the
        # minimum cameras under three exclusive conditions:
        #   a = this node has a camera
        #   b = this node is covered (by itself or a child) but has no camera
        #   c = this node is NOT covered (allowed only if its parent will cover it)
        INF = float('inf')
        def dp(node):
            if node is None:
                # absent node: no camera, treat as covered, cannot 'need cover'
                return (INF, 0, 0)
            la, lb, lc = dp(node.left)
            ra, rb, rc = dp(node.right)
            # camera here: children may be in any state, +1
            a = 1 + min(la, lb, lc) + min(ra, rb, rc)
            # covered without a camera here: at least one child has a camera,
            # the other is covered (has camera or covered)
            b = min(la + min(ra, rb), ra + min(la, lb))
            # not covered here: both children must be covered without a camera
            c = lb + rb
            return (a, b, c)
        a, b, c = dp(root)
        return min(a, b)
